BTC-USD.reasoning: BTC ranks #1 in momentum quality. Daily ROC fully accelerating: 5d=+1.67%, 10d=+6.47%, 21d=+14.18% — all positive and expanding across all three timeframes. EMAs are aligned in uptrend (10>21>50). Daily RSI at 73 trending upward. MACD histogram strongly positive (+634) and expanding. Bollinger position at 0.83 (upper band, demand zone). MFI=82.5 confirms institutional buying pressure. The hourly frame shows a short-term pullback: EMA stack inverted, hourly RSI=31.2 (oversold), MACD slightly negative — this represents a tactical buying opportunity within the dominant daily uptrend rather than a reversal. Hourly volume is 1.46x average on the down-move, consistent with institutional accumulation at the $74-75K dip. BTC dominance at ~58% (multi-month high) confirms capital is rotating INTO BTC. Web catalysts are strong: $921M ETF inflows over 5-day window, BlackRock IBIT $284M single-day, Strategy treasury at 781K BTC, whale accumulation at 30-year high (270K BTC added), exchange reserves at 9-year low. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve design due July 2026 provides medium-term upside catalyst. Key risk: April 15 exchange inflow spike of 11,000 BTC/hour at $75K resistance suggests some large-holder distribution near current levels; daily RSI at 73 is approaching overbought. Current pullback to $74,557 from $77,127 peak is a healthy retest of the 23.6% Fibonacci level ($74,439). Entry on momentum continuation above the hourly downtrend. Confidence capped at 78 given RSI proximity to overbought and known distribution signals at resistance.

ETH-USD.reasoning: ETH shows strong multi-week momentum but recent deceleration creates a mixed signal. Daily ROC: 5d=-0.83% (negative — short-term momentum has turned), 10d=+7.34%, 21d=+17.99%. The 21d ROC is the strongest in the group, but the 5d turning negative indicates the momentum train may be losing steam at current levels. Daily RSI=67.1, MACD histogram positive (+19.85) and expanding — still constructive on the daily. ADX=40.4 is the strongest trend reading in the group. Daily EMAs aligned uptrend (10>21>50). However, current price $2,281 has fallen below all recent closes ($2,323-$2,421), sitting below the 10 EMA ($2,310). Hourly frame is distinctly bearish: EMA downtrend, RSI=25.7 (oversold near capitulation), MACD histogram negative, volume at 0.59x average (lack of buying conviction). Z-score hourly = -1.45, price 2.46% below hourly 50 SMA. The divergence between strong 21d trend and negative 5d ROC plus weak hourly internals is the classic momentum deceleration signal this strategy avoids. However, the 25.7 hourly RSI suggests oversold conditions that could spark a snap-back recovery. Web: ETH ETF 6 consecutive days of inflows, Glamsterdam hard fork (parallel execution, 78% fee reduction) is a major H1 2026 catalyst; ETH staking ETF live. DeFi risk: $5.4B Aave rsETH exploit overhang suppresses near-term confidence. Neutral stance until 5d ROC turns positive and hourly EMAs recover.

SOL-USD.reasoning: SOL ranks last in momentum quality — weakest setup in the group by a significant margin. Daily ROC: 5d=-0.52%, 10d=+4.38%, 21d=+5.11%. The 5d ROC is negative and the 21d ROC of +5.11% is by far the weakest among the five tickers. ADX=19.5 is the lowest in the group, indicating a weak/trendless environment. EMAs are NOT aligned in an uptrend — the 50 EMA ($87.41) is ABOVE current price ($84.47), a bearish configuration. This is the only asset where price is below the 50 EMA. MACD histogram is barely positive (+0.74), effectively flat. Stochastic K=63.5 but D=79.1 — bearish crossover occurring. Hourly frame confirms weakness: EMA full downtrend (10<21<50), RSI=31.2, MACD histogram negative, but critically volume is only 0.22x average — the lowest across all five tickers. This absence of buying interest on the dip is a significant bearish signal; compare to BTC where hourly volume is 1.46x average. Z-score hourly = -1.22, price 1.86% below 50 SMA. Web research: technical analysts are warning of breakdown with key support at $78-82 under pressure; SOL dropped 3%+ on April 19 leading altcoin losses. The $270M Drift exploit overhang creates institutional hesitation. While Firedancer H2 2026 and Alpenglow are positive long-term catalysts, they are not near-term momentum drivers. Whale accumulation signals are mixed; spot buying demand collapsed ~80% since March 22. BTC dominance rising suppresses altcoin rotation. Momentum says avoid or short.

BNB-USD.reasoning: BNB presents a positive but decelerating momentum profile. Daily ROC: 5d=+2.48%, 10d=+4.85%, 21d=+3.21%. Crucially, the pattern is NOT accelerating — the 21d ROC is lower than the 10d ROC, indicating that while near-term momentum is positive, the broader 3-week trend is less vigorous than the past 2 weeks. This deceleration pattern is the key concern. Daily RSI=63.5 (bullish but not extreme), MACD histogram +5.37 (positive and expanding — one of the better signals here). Bollinger position=0.83. Stochastic: K=70.1, D=88.0 — bearish crossover forming (K rolling below D). EMAs are NOT aligned bullish: 50 EMA ($634.40) is above current price ($621.46). Current price is below all three major EMAs on the daily (10 EMA $621.75, 21 EMA $618.31, 50 EMA $634.40) — near the 10 EMA, just barely below. Hourly frame is the least negative among non-BTC assets: RSI=42.3 (neutral), MACD histogram +0.38 (slightly positive), EMAs in mild downtrend. Hourly volume is extremely thin at 0.08x average — caution on reliability of signals. Web: $1.32B BNB burn on April 15 is a confirmed positive catalyst. BNB Chain Osaka/Mendel hard fork on April 28 is an imminent technical upgrade catalyst. YZi Labs $1B ecosystem fund and Kyrgyzstan CBDC are positive fundamental developments. However, DOJ probe overhang and structural decline in CEX volumes (-48% from October 2025 peak) weigh on the long-term BNB utility thesis. Neutral pending resolution of the EMA alignment issue and momentum deceleration pattern.

XRP-USD.reasoning: XRP ranks #2 in momentum quality, just behind BTC. Daily ROC: 5d=+4.21%, 10d=+6.88%, 21d=+7.64%. The pattern is positive across all three timeframes, and notably the 5d ROC (+4.21%) is showing near-term strength even within the broader bullish trend. Volume on the daily is above average (1.10x) — the only non-BTC asset where daily volume confirms momentum. Bollinger position=0.90 — highest in the group, indicating price is testing the upper band, showing strong momentum. Daily RSI=65.5 (bullish, not yet overbought). MACD histogram is very thin (+0.016) suggesting MACD momentum is early in development, not over-extended. ADX=31.0 (moderate-strong trend). Stochastic K=67.9 with D=82.6 — K is well below D here which could signal near-term pullback. The EMAs present a mixed picture: 10 EMA ($1.4017) > 21 EMA ($1.3834) — short-term aligned, but 50 EMA ($1.4115) is above current price ($1.41) — showing some resistance just overhead. Hourly is oversold: RSI=29.4, EMA downtrend, but hourly volume is 1.16x average (above average — second best after BTC), suggesting active participation even in the pullback. The $1.41 current price vs. $1.477 recent high represents a shallow pullback within positive daily momentum. Web catalysts are among the strongest of any ticker: 7 spot XRP ETF decisions expected Q2 2026 (binary massive catalyst), SEC/CFTC reclassified XRP as commodity (regulatory clarity), CLARITY Act Senate vote expected May 2026, Rakuten Wallet integration for 44M users, Ripple/Convera $190B cross-border payment partnership, whale accumulation at 10-month high. However, XRP is down 42% YTD and 48% from ATH — this is a momentum RECOVERY trade, not a trend continuation from highs. Confidence at 68 (below BTC's 78) reflecting the thinner MACD confirmation and the overhead 50 EMA resistance.
